reasons_to_visit.details: By mid-March, Parc des Bastions, the Botanical Gardens, and the Treille promenade are flecked with crocuses and the first cherry blossoms. Days are noticeably longer and Mont Blanc views, when clear, are stunning.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Salon de l'Auto / Motor-Show Replacement Events
reasons_to_visit.details: Although the historic Geneva International Motor Show has paused, March still brings replacement automotive, design, and trade events at Palexpo, with hotel demand spiking on those weekends.
Sunny days, soft afternoon snow, and longer light make March one of the best months for skiing in Chamonix, Verbier, and the Portes du Soleil — from Geneva you can be on the lifts within two hours.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Easter & Early School Holidays (Late March in Some Years)
reasons_to_visit.details: When Easter falls late March, family-friendly chocolatiers around Rue du Marché and Carouge fill with elaborate Easter eggs and tasting events.
Bains des Pâquis daytime crowds return on sunny weekends, the first lake-shore runners are out by 7am, and Quai du Mont-Blanc terraces start setting tables back outside.
Outside Palexpo trade-fair weekends, hotel rates are softer than in summer and autumn, with strong availability at lakefront properties.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: March in Geneva can swing from 18°C and sunny to snow flurries and cold rain in 48 hours. Pack for both.
When a major trade show is at Palexpo (near the airport), hotel rates can triple and restaurant tables disappear with no warning.
CGN's full summer schedule (with belle-époque paddle steamers and lake tours) typically starts only in April. March options are short shuttle routes.
Higher Jura and Salève hiking trails can be muddy, icy, or snowbound until end-of-month. Stick to lower lakefront and vineyard walks.
Early tree pollen (hazel, alder) is active by mid-March, which can affect sensitive travellers.
Clocks jump forward in late March, briefly shortening morning light and disrupting cross-border train schedules.

weather.precipitation: Mix of cold spells and milder Atlantic systems. Frost mornings still possible; first 15°C+ afternoons appear on sunny days.
weather.general_tips: Layered clothing with windproof shell. Carry a light scarf for evenings.

tourism_level.crowds: Quiet on non-trade-fair weekdays; sharp spikes when Palexpo events run.
tourism_level.pricing: Mostly low except around Palexpo events. Book lakefront hotels early when trade-show dates are known.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Late winter shading into early spring. The first warm sunny afternoons feel transformative.

events_holidays.name: Palexpo Trade Shows
events_holidays.description: Various major fairs (replacement for Motor Show, watch industry meetings, design events) drive significant visitor traffic.
events_holidays.notes: Hotels near the airport and Place des Nations fill first. Cornavin trains stay calm.

events_holidays.name: Internationales Day of the Francophonie
events_holidays.description: Geneva, as a UN city and home to OIF cultural events, hosts concerts, literary nights, and cinema programming celebrating Francophone culture.
events_holidays.notes: Many events are free and held in libraries, cafés, and the Maison Internationale.

unique_experiences.activity: Parc des Bastions & Reformation Wall Walk
unique_experiences.where: Place de Neuve to Promenade des Bastions
unique_experiences.description: Catch the first spring colour in the historic park beneath the Old Town, with the giant outdoor chess sets back in play and the Reformation Wall's five-metre figures of Calvin, Farel, Bèze, and Knox staring out from the rampart.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Combine with a coffee on Place de Neuve and a wander up Rue de la Cité.

unique_experiences.where: Verbier — train to Le Châble, then bus/cable car
unique_experiences.description: Verbier's altitude (1,500–3,300m) keeps snow superb in March, and longer days make for relaxed sunny lunches on mountain terraces.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Train Geneva → Martigny → Le Châble, then cable car. Allow 2.5 hours each way; many start with a 6am train.

unique_experiences.where: Patek Philippe Museum + Plainpalais watch-industry walk
unique_experiences.description: Combine the museum visit with a walk past historic watch-industry buildings on Rue de Carouge and Boulevard de Saint-Georges.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Patek closed Sun/Mon. Several boutiques offer brief atelier visits by appointment.

unique_experiences.where: Quai du Général-Guisan, Bains des Pâquis, Quai du Mont-Blanc
unique_experiences.description: The Jet d'Eau typically resumes daily operations through March (weather permitting). Loop the lake mouth via the bridges for a 5–7km run with classic views.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Café stops at Pâquis and Eaux-Vives. Hat and gloves still wise early mornings.

weather.precipitation: Warmer and longer days. Occasional sharp cold snaps possible but proper winter days become rare. Atlantic rain bands occasionally pass through.
weather.general_tips: First terrace season begins on sunny weekends. Daylight saving begins in late March — pleasant evenings extend to 20:00.

tourism_level.crowds: Building gently — weekenders from Zurich, Lyon, and Paris start arriving for lake views.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Energetic transition into spring; terraces fill, lake runners multiply, Mont Blanc reasserts itself on clear days.

events_holidays.name: Daylight Saving Time Begins
events_holidays.description: Clocks move forward one hour, giving Geneva long bright evenings.
events_holidays.notes: Cross-border train schedules briefly disrupted; double-check connections to France.

events_holidays.name: Easter (when falling in March)
events_holidays.description: Public holidays Good Friday and Easter Monday — many shops closed, but restaurants and museums open and chocolatiers in full display.
events_holidays.notes: Chocolatiers Auer, du Rhône, and Stettler unveil their Easter collections.

events_holidays.name: Spring Programme at Bâtiment des Forces Motrices
events_holidays.description: The Grand Théâtre de Genève and BFM often programme major opera and orchestral performances heading into spring.
events_holidays.notes: Book ahead; reduced-price last-minute tickets sometimes released.

unique_experiences.where: Pier at Quai du Mont-Blanc → Yvoire (France)
unique_experiences.description: As CGN's spring schedule expands, hop a ferry across to medieval Yvoire on the French shore, with cobbled lanes, flower-draped walls, and lake-view restaurants opening for the season.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Return crossings reduced compared to summer — check timetable.

unique_experiences.description: Take the 50-minute train to Vevey or Lutry and walk a flat-to-gentle section of the Lavaux UNESCO vineyards, with terraces newly pruned and the lake glittering below.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Some cellar doors begin opening for weekend tastings late March. Trains every 30 minutes from Cornavin.

unique_experiences.description: With longer evenings, ride up at 18:00 and walk the plateau for sunset views across the city, lake, and Mont Blanc lit pink.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Last cable car down typically 22:00 in spring. Bring layers — temperatures drop fast.

unique_experiences.where: Auer (Rue de Rive), Du Rhône (Rue de la Confédération), Stettler (Rive)
unique_experiences.description: Geneva's chocolatiers compete on Easter window displays — towering chocolate eggs, hand-painted figurines, and pralinés in spring flavours.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Buy early in the week before Easter to avoid Saturday queues.
